title: cni EPERM as non-root on centos7
body:
<!--
If you are reporting a new issue, make sure that we do not have any duplicates
already open. You can ensure this by searching the issue list for this
repository. If there is a duplicate, please close your issue and add a comment
to the existing issue instead.

If you suspect your issue is a bug, please edit your issue description to
include the BUG REPORT INFORMATION shown below. If you fail to provide this
information within 7 days, we cannot debug your issue and will close it. We
will, however, reopen it if you later provide the information.

---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST
-->

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**
1. yum install buildah
2. https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/vbatts/shadow-utils-newxidmap/
3. `buildah bud .` or `f=$(buildah from fedora); buildah run $f echo farts` as a non-root user on centos7


**Describe the results you received:**
```
[vbatts@beater] /tmp/tmp.Tz1V5MFbsc$ buildah bud -t farts .
STEP 1: FROM fedora
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:565884f490d9ec697e519c57d55d09e268542ef2c1340fd63262751fa308f047
 82.90 MiB / 82.90 MiB [====================================================] 3s
Copying config sha256:c582c1438f27b3775e2534abc82d14974ecb00c2c53161d05ec73a73d35e1235
 2.29 KiB / 2.29 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
STEP 2: RUN echo farts
error running container: error configuring network list if0 for [/bin/sh -c echo farts]: failed to create bridge "cni0": could not add "cni0": operation not permitted
error building at step {Env:[PATH=/usr/local/sbin:/usr/local/bin:/usr/sbin:/usr/bin:/sbin:/bin DISTTAG=f28container FGC=f28 FBR=f28] Command:run Args:[echo farts] Fla
e:RUN echo farts Original:RUN echo farts}: exit status 1
ERRO[0012] exit status 1
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
basic build

**Output of `rpm -q buildah` or `apt list buildah`:**

```
buildah-1.2-2.gitbe87762.el7.x86_64
```

**Output of `buildah version`:**

```
Version:         1.2
Go Version:      go1.9.4
Image Spec:      1.0.0
Runtime Spec:    1.0.0
CNI Spec:        0.3.1
libcni Version:  v0.6.0
Git Commit:      be87762
Built:           Tue Aug 21 10:38:24 2018
OS/Arch:         linux/amd64
```

**Output of `cat /etc/*release`:**

```
CentOS Linux release 7.5.1804 (Core) 
NAME="CentOS Linux"
VERSION="7 (Core)"
ID="centos"
ID_LIKE="rhel fedora"
VERSION_ID="7"
PRETTY_NAME="CentOS Linux 7 (Core)"
ANSI_COLOR="0;31"
CPE_NAME="cpe:/o:centos:centos:7"
HOME_URL="https://www.centos.org/"
BUG_REPORT_URL="https://bugs.centos.org/"

CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT="CentOS-7"
CENTOS_MANTISBT_PROJECT_VERSION="7"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT="centos"
REDHAT_SUPPORT_PRODUCT_VERSION="7"

CentOS Linux release 7.5.1804 (Core) 
CentOS Linux release 7.5.1804 (Core) 
```

**Output of `uname -a`:**

```
Linux beater.usersys.redhat.com 3.10.0-862.14.4.el7.x86_64 #1 SMP Wed Sep 26 15:12:11 UTC 2018 x86_64 x86_64 x86_64 GNU/Linux
```

comments.body:
If you run it with --net=host, does it work?
@giuseppe Do we need to integrate with slirp4netns to make this work like we do on podman?

I think it is just a too old buildah.  I've followed the guide from https://copr.fedorainfracloud.org/coprs/vbatts/shadow-utils-newxidmap/ to configure a rootless user and I see buildah-1.2-2.gitbe87762.el7.x86_64 on Centos 7 failing when used in rootless mode.  Upstream version works fine.
Since anyway rootless buildah is not supported on RHEL 7 (due to the lack of newuidmap/newgidmap), should we get an updated version in a copr as well?
If you want to protect abstract sockets on the host from malicious Dockerfiles, I suggest using non-host network namespace with slirp4netns.
Well SELinux should protect you also from those, but I agree we need to get slirp4netns into RHEL and Centos.

> If you run it with --net=host, does it work?

```shell
$ buildah bud --net=host -t farts .
STEP 1: FROM fedora
STEP 2: RUN echo farts
farts
STEP 3: COMMIT containers-storage:[vfs@/home/vbatts/.local/share/containers/storage+/run/user/1000/run]localhost/farts:latest
Getting image source signatures
Skipping fetch of repeat blob sha256:108b1c56d076789d94323e2bcaa7eb926485f8fc841d6c08335f39412d94b71c
Copying blob sha256:4f4fb700ef54461cfa02571ae0db9a0dc1e0cdb5577484a6d75e68dc38e8acc1
 32 B / 32 B [==============================================================] 0s
Copying config sha256:de9eaa7d6de3dba1b8400ca447ddc5f039b372e30f2797f9ab428c6218c761a2
 1.56 KiB / 1.56 KiB [======================================================] 0s
Writing manifest to image destination
Storing signatures
--> de9eaa7d6de3dba1b8400ca447ddc5f039b372e30f2797f9ab428c6218c761a2
```
I surely hope we're not abandoning centos7/RHEL7 for this yet. It's a current demand.
No I should say not.  RHEL7 pays the bills.  :^)

> @lsm5 Is slirp4netns available on RHEL7?

Nope :\

>
>
> @lsm5 <https://github.com/lsm5> Is slirp4netns available on RHEL7?
>
> Nope :\
>
>
It compiles fine and seems to work ok on centos7

>
>
